prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic photograph of deep interstellar space, 16:9 aspect ratio. Near-total blackness. The scene is dominated by an enormous rift torn open in the fabric of spacetime itself — not a clean geometric portal, but a raw, asymmetric tear in reality, as if the universe cracked open. The edges of the rift shimmer with hair-thin veins of molten gold light bleeding into the void, like liquid gold capillaries spreading through obsidian glass. Through the rift, another dimension is barely visible — a vast impossible landscape of inverted light, crystalline structures stretching into infinity, faint violet-black auroras pulsing in silence. The rift is positioned slightly left of center. The surrounding space is almost featureless — pure black (#080808) with scattered diamond-point stars, each one razor-sharp, some with faint gold halos. In the far distance, a single massive nebula cloud hangs like smoke — nearly invisible, rendered in deep burgundy (#1A0510) and charcoal, threaded with microscopic filaments of warm gold dust. The foreground has imperceptible cosmic particulate drifting through — tiny motes of light caught in nothing. The entire image feels like it was captured by the James Webb Space Telescope pointed at something that should not exist — clinical, scientific, terrifying in its beauty. The color palette is exclusively: pure black, near-black, deep violet-black, burgundy-black, and warm antiqued gold (#C9A84C) for the rift edges only. Everything else lives in the shadows. Hasselblad medium format quality, 35mm focal length, f/2.8, ISO 50, 120-second exposure. Absolute sharpness. No glow, no bloom, no lens flare, no fog. The blacks must be crushingly deep. This is not fantasy — this is a photograph of something real and unknowable. No text. No symbols. No geometric overlays. The rift itself IS the geometry.
